name: Log4Shell
description: Apache Log4j2 JNDI features used in configuration, log messages, and parameters do not protect against attacker controlled LDAP and other JNDI related endpoints.
remediation: Update Log4j to >= 2.15.0 or set formatMsgNoLookups=true

name: DOM-based XSS via innerHTML
description: Data is read from a user-controllable source and passed to a dangerous sink like innerHTML.
remediation: Use textContent instead of innerHTML, or sanitize using DOMPurify.

name: Missing SameSite Cookie Attribute
description: Session cookies missing the SameSite attribute default to allowing cross-site requests in older browsers, or Lax in newer ones, opening vectors for CSRF.
remediation: Set SameSite=Strict or SameSite=Lax on all session cookies.

name: JWT None Algorithm
description: The server accepts JWTs signed with the 'none' algorithm, allowing attackers to forge tokens.
remediation: Enforce strict algorithm checking and reject 'none' algorithm in JWT parsing libraries.

name: Unauthenticated Actuator Endpoints
description: Spring Boot actuator endpoints (/actuator/env, /actuator/heapdump) are exposed without authentication.
remediation: Disable sensitive actuator endpoints or protect them with Spring Security.

name: Heartbleed
description: The (1) TLS and (2) DTLS implementations in OpenSSL do not properly handle Heartbeat Extension packets.

name: Reflected XSS in URL Parameters
description: User input from the URL query string is reflected directly into the HTML response without sanitization.
remediation: Context-aware HTML encoding of all user-supplied data before reflection.

name: Insecure Direct Object Reference
description: API endpoints accessing resources via predictable IDs (/api/users/123) without checking if the current user owns the resource.
remediation: Implement strict authorization checks verifying ownership at the controller level.

name: API Key Exposure in Client Source
description: Sensitive API keys (AWS, Stripe, etc.) are hardcoded in client-side JavaScript.
remediation: Move secrets to backend environment variables and proxy API calls.

name: Missing Strict-Transport-Security
description: Site lacks HSTS header, allowing active network attackers to downgrade connections to plaintext HTTP.
